<!-- status autogenerated section -->
# Ambient Weather Receiver

The Ambient Weather Receiver hosts an HTTP server that ingests metrics pushed by Ambient Weather Wi-Fi
consoles configured in "custom server" mode (the WS View / Awnet "Customized" upload screen). Weather
readings arrive as URL query parameters in the AMBWeather format and are converted into OpenTelemetry
gauge metrics. Measurements are converted to canonical SI/UCUM units, except for relative-humidity-style
percentages which are passed through unchanged.

This receiver is fully local: the weather console sends data directly to the collector over the
LAN, with no dependency on the [Ambient Weather](https://ambientweather.com/) cloud and no API
credentials.

## Configuration

| Field          | Default          | Description |
|----------------|------------------|-------------|
| `endpoint`     | `localhost:6255` | Host:port the HTTP server listens on. The console is a separate device on the LAN, so real deployments must bind a routable address (e.g. `0.0.0.0:6255`) rather than `localhost`. |
| `path`         | `/data/report/`  | URL path the console is configured to upload to. |
| `station_name` | (unset)          | Optional friendly name applied as the `ambientweather.station.name` resource attribute. |

The receiver also accepts the standard [`confighttp.ServerConfig`](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector/blob/main/config/confighttp/README.md)
server settings (TLS, CORS, etc.) and the standard `metrics` / `resource_attributes` enablement
toggles documented in [documentation.md](./documentation.md).

### Example

```yaml
receivers:
  ambientweather:
    endpoint: 0.0.0.0:6255
    path: /data/report/
    station_name: backyard
```

## Console setup

In the WS View / Awnet app, open the console's **Customized** upload screen and set:

- **Protocol Type**: `AMBWeather`
- **Server IP / Hostname**: the collector host's LAN address
- **Path**: `/data/report/` (must match `path`)
- **Port**: `6255` (must match `endpoint`)
- **Upload Interval**: as desired (e.g. 60s)

## Notes

- All metrics are gauges representing the latest reading. Rainfall accumulation windows (hourly,
  daily, weekly, monthly, yearly, 24-hour, event, total) are reported by the device and exposed as a
  single `ambientweather.rainfall` metric distinguished by the `rainfall.period` attribute.
- The point timestamp is taken from the console's `dateutc` field when present, falling back to the
  receive time otherwise.
- Multi-channel add-on sensors are distinguished by the `sensor` attribute (`"1"`..`"10"`); the
  primary console sensors use `"outdoor"` / `"indoor"`.
